tipRules.tip: KVD: 'In cold water, I use a solid pull a couple times and pause briefly — point the rod at the bait for slack and enhanced action. Pull the bait short distances vs. jerking hard to run 3-4 feet deeper. One second pause between pulls is plenty for me.'

KVD: 'I prefer real bright colors — sexy shad and chartreuse sexy shad — even in clear water. It's about getting the fish to see the bait when they're far away. I like 3-4 feet of clarity minimum, crystal clear is ideal.'

knowledge.insight: KVD: 'Cold water jerkbait: solid pull (not snap), pause one second with rod pointed at bait for slack. By pulling short distances the bait runs 3-4 feet deeper. I still cover a lot of water by fishing parallel to edges — keeps the lure in the productive zone throughout the retrieve.'

KVD: 'As water temp drops, a jerkbait that floats in summer will sink in cold water. Switch to three No. 4 Mustad KVD Trebles and test balance — a little sink is fine but nothing rapid or unnatural. 6-10 medium heavy rod, 10lb fluorocarbon for long casts in clear water.'

KVD: 'Cold water jerkbait targets: drop-offs, edges, weedlines, bridge corners, riprap, bluff ends. Fish parallel to these edges and make multiple casts to high-percentage spots like laydown tips or grass points.'
